finding.summary: The 1/3-octave spectrum tilts steadily downward from low to high, with the 100 Hz band 12 dB louder than the 4 kHz band — a dark, bass-heavy balance that does not match the typical clean-electric-guitar profile, which usually carries presence energy around 1–4 kHz. Level metrics are otherwise healthy: RMS -18 dB, sample peak -6.0 dB, true peak -5.5 dB, integrated LUFS -20, crest factor 12 dB, and the capture is not diluted by silence (5%). The track has no FX, so the tonal shaping needed to correct this tilt is not being applied here.
finding.probable_cause: Either the source capture is dark (neck pickup warmth, distant/dark mic placement, room tone) or the EQ that clean guitars normally receive (a high-pass around 80–120 Hz and a presence lift near 2–4 kHz) has been deferred to the parent 'Guitar Bus'. The empty FX chain on this track confirms that no corrective processing is currently applied at the track level.
finding.confidence_reason: Only three 1/3-octave bands were supplied, so the tilt is clear but its exact origin (low-end buildup vs. high-end roll-off vs. an intentionally warm tone) cannot be pinpointed without finer spectral resolution; no stereo/phase data was captured, and the absence of FX on the track could reflect deliberate bus-based processing rather than an oversight.

evidence_refs.description: 100 Hz at -18 dB — the strongest band in the supplied spectrum.
1000 Hz at -24 dB — 6 dB below 100 Hz.
4000 Hz at -30 dB — 12 dB below 100 Hz and 6 dB below 1 kHz.
Empty FX chain — no EQ or other tonal processing exists on this track to address the tilt.
0.05 — capture is essentially live audio, so the spectrum is not biased by silence.
Routes to 'Guitar Bus' — tonal shaping may be intended to happen upstream at the bus.

proposal.reason: The only supported operation that could address the dark tonal balance is set_fx_param on an EQ, but the track's FX chain is empty, so no parameter exists to adjust. Volume (0.0 dB), pan (0.0 normalized), sample peak (-6.0 dB), and true peak (-5.5 dB) are all within normal ranges, leaving nothing on this track to safely change with the available operations. The corrective move belongs either on the parent 'Guitar Bus' or via adding an EQ to this track — neither of which the supported operations can perform.
